title: Configure the Uniface Web Application Server on iSeries
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
Deploying Server Pages and Web Services on iSeries
Configure the Uniface Web Application Server on iSeries
text: Configure the Uniface Web Application Server on iSeries To be able to deploy Uniface web applications on the iSeries, you must configure the Uniface Web Application Server so that it can correctly locate and run web components. To do so, you need to ensure that assignment files of the Uniface Router, Uniface Server, and client assignment file match the Web Application Server defined in the web.xml file. In the web.xml file, the last element in the connection string is the UST used by the Uniface Router to start a Uniface Server. It is set to wasv by default. In the Uniface Router's assignment file, the [SERVER] section of the Uniface Router's assignment file has an entry for wasv , such as the following: wasv = userver -asn=wasv.asn Copy The Uniface Router (UROUTER) starts a new Uniface Server (USERVER) instance when it receives its first request for UST wasv . The USERVER assignment file wasv.asn contains the settings and paths or file redirections required to correctly run the web application components. The client assignment file must define a path to the iSeries where the components will be executed. The path must use the same UST wasv that is used in web.xml . Note: The following instructions are for a test service called websrv.svc , which will run on the iSeries and be called from a form running in the Uniface IDE on a Windows client. Edit the client assignment file, in this case ide.asn For this example, if you have $TEST_MODE_COMPONENTS in your assignment file, temporarily comment it out. Add an entry to the [SERVICES_EXEC] section to direct the execution of the websrv.svc service to the UST wasv : [SERVICES_EXEC] WEBSRV $WASV:WEBSRV Copy Add an entry to the [PATHS] section to define the path to the WASV Uniface Server on the iSeries: [PATHS] $WASV TCP: iSeriesNetworkName + Port | User | Password |wasv Copy The contents of this $WASV setting must be the same as that specified in the web.xml (see Configure the WRD and Create a wrd.war File on iSeries ), except for the following: Replace UV8: by TCP: If you used the literal ‘ localhost ’ in web.xml , replace it with the actual network name of your iSeries machine. For example: $WASV TCP:myAS400+13001|userver|userver|wasv Copy Edit the assignment file of the Uniface Server ( wasv.asn ). Add an entry to the [RESOURCES] section to specify the location of the UARs containing the components. For example: [RESOURCES] ; if the UAR is in member MY_UAR of file UAR: UNIFxxxx/my_uar.uar ; if the UAR is in the IFS: IFS:/home/username/uars/my_uar.uar Copy You should now be able to create and test the websrv.svc service, as described in Create and Run a Test Service on iSeries and Run the Service as a Web Service .
